02A. Maternal Effects and Reproduction: Days-to-Hatch

Elliott Schmidt

2026-04-11

Load libraries

library(tidyverse) # data manipulation
library(ggpubr) # producing data exploratory plots
library(modelsummary) # descriptive data 
library(glmmTMB) # running generalised mixed models 
library(DHARMa) # model diagnostics 
library(performance) # model diagnostics  
library(ggeffects) # partial effect plots 
library(car) # running Anova on model 
library(emmeans) # post-hoc analysis  
library(MuMIn) # model comparisons
library(lme4)  #blups
library(patchwork) #aligning plots

Import data

setwd("C:/Users/elliott/OneDrive/jc527762/PhD dissertation/Data/Chapter3_Heritability_ARCHIVED")
egg <- read_csv("import_data/egg_size_data_2022_2023.csv") |> 
  mutate(across(1:14,factor))  |> 
  select(!c(NOTES,...18, IMAGE)) 

reprod.data <- read_csv("import_data/clutch_data_2022_2023.csv") |> 
  mutate(across(c(1:7,16,23), factor))  

m2.5 <- read_csv("import_data/2-5_month_size_data_2022_2023.csv") |> 
  mutate(across(1:15,factor)) |> 
  mutate(STANDARD_LENGTH =LENGTH, 
         .keep = "unused") |> 
  select(!(NOTES)) |> 
  select(1:15,"STANDARD_LENGTH","MASS")|> 
  group_by(CLUTCH_NUMBER) |> 
  mutate(DENSITY = n()) |> 
  ungroup()

adult <- read_csv("import_data/adult_size_2022_2023.csv") |> 
  mutate(across(1:3,factor), 
         MALE = FISH_ID, 
         FEMALE = FISH_ID, 
         POPULATION = str_sub(FISH_ID, 2,4), 
         POPULATION = case_when(POPULATION == "ARL" ~ "Arlington Reef", 
                                POPULATION == "SUD" ~ "Sudbury Reef",
                                POPULATION == "VLA" ~ "Vlassof cay",
                                POPULATION == "PRE" ~ "Pretty patches", 
                                TRUE ~ POPULATION)) |> 
  left_join(select(m2.5, c("MALE","TEMPERATURE")), 
             by="MALE") |> 
  left_join(select(m2.5, c("FEMALE","TEMPERATURE")), 
             by="FEMALE") |>
  distinct() |> 
  mutate(TEMPERATURE = coalesce(TEMPERATURE.x, TEMPERATURE.y)) |> 
  drop_na(TEMPERATURE) |> 
  select(-c("TEMPERATURE.x","TEMPERATURE.y"))

Data manipulation

m2.5_df_all <- m2.5 |> 
  left_join(select(adult, c("MALE", "SL", "MASS")), 
            by ="MALE") |> 
  mutate(SL_MALE =SL, 
         MASS_MALE =MASS.y, 
         .keep = "unused") |>
  left_join(select(adult, c("FEMALE", "SL", "MASS")), 
            by ="FEMALE") |> 
  mutate(SL_FEMALE =SL, 
         MASS_FEMALE =MASS, 
         .keep ="unused") 

m2.5_df <- m2.5_df_all |>
  group_by(CLUTCH_NUMBER) |> 
  mutate(MEDIAN_STANDARD_LENGTH = median(STANDARD_LENGTH)) |>
  drop_na(MEDIAN_STANDARD_LENGTH) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(-c("STANDARD_LENGTH","MASS.x")) |> 
  distinct()

clutches <- reprod.data |> filter(PROJECT_CODE %in% c("1","3","5","7")) |>
  select(CLUTCH_NUMBER)

egg_df_all <- egg |> 
  left_join(select(adult, c("MALE", "SL", "MASS")), 
            by ="MALE")  |> 
  mutate(SL_MALE =SL, 
         MASS_MALE =MASS, 
         .keep = "unused") |>
  left_join(select(adult, c("FEMALE", "SL", "MASS")), 
            by ="FEMALE") |> 
  mutate(SL_FEMALE =SL, 
         MASS_FEMALE =MASS, 
         .keep ="unused") |> 
  mutate(SL_MIDPOINT = (SL_MALE+SL_FEMALE)/2, 
         MASS_MIDPOINT = (MASS_MALE+MASS_FEMALE)/2) |> 
  left_join(select(reprod.data, c("CLUTCH_NUMBER","EGG_COUNT","HATCHING_TIME")), by="CLUTCH_NUMBER")

egg_df <- egg_df_all |>
  group_by(CLUTCH_NUMBER) |> 
  mutate(MEDIAN_HATCHING_TIME = median(HATCHING_TIME)) |> 
  drop_na(MEDIAN_HATCHING_TIME) |>
  ungroup()  |> 
  select(-c("EGG_SIZE","SAMPLE")) |> 
  distinct() |> 
  mutate(MASS_MIDPOINT =coalesce(MASS_MIDPOINT, MASS_MALE), 
         SL_MIDPOINT =coalesce(SL_MIDPOINT, SL_MALE), 
         SL_FEMALE =coalesce(SL_FEMALE, SL_MALE))  # done for two individuals
  
egg_df <- clutches |> 
  inner_join(egg_df, by="CLUTCH_NUMBER") |> 
  drop_na(HATCHING_TIME) |> 
  droplevels()

egg_df_all <- clutches |> 
  inner_join(egg_df_all, by="CLUTCH_NUMBER") |> 
  droplevels()

Exploratory data analysis

plot1 <- ggplot(egg_df, aes(x=TEMPERATURE, y=HATCHING_TIME, color=TEMPERATURE, fill=TEMPERATURE)) +
  geom_dotplot(binaxis="y") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#69d7d8","#ff9c56", "#903146")) + 
  ggtitle("HATCHING TIME") +
  theme_classic(); plot1

plot2 <- ggplot(egg_df, aes(x=MASS_FEMALE, y=HATCHING_TIME, color=TEMPERATURE, fill=TEMPERATURE)) +
  geom_point() + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#69d7d8","#ff9c56", "#903146")) + 
  ggtitle("HATCHING TIME") +
  theme_classic(); plot2

Descriptive statistics

datasummary(Factor(POPULATION) ~ Factor(TEMPERATURE), 
            data=egg_df, 
            fmt = "%.0f")
POPULATION 27 28.5 30
Arlington Reef 7 6 8
Pretty Patches 3 3 5
Sudbury Reef 4 2 2
Vlassof Cay 4 2 4 
sample_size_hatchtime <- egg_df |> 
  group_by(TEMPERATURE, MALE, FEMALE, POPULATION, CLUTCH_NUMBER, HATCHING_TIME) |> 
  summarise(n =n(), .groups = "drop"); sample_size_hatchtime
write.csv(sample_size_hatchtime, "../../sample_size_hatchtime.csv")

Fit models [random factors]

modelNULL <- glmmTMB(HATCHING_TIME ~ 1, 
                  family=gaussian(),
                  data =egg_df)

model1 <- glmmTMB(HATCHING_TIME ~ (1|FEMALE), 
                  family=gaussian(),
                  data = egg_df)

AIC(modelNULL, model1, k=2) 
BIC(modelNULL, model1)

Fit fixed factors

Main hypothesis

model1a <- glmmTMB(HATCHING_TIME ~ TEMPERATURE + (1|FEMALE), 
                    family=gaussian(link="identity"), 
                    data=egg_df)

Model validation

DHARMa

model1a |> 
  simulateResiduals(plot=TRUE)  

## Object of Class DHARMa with simulated residuals based on 250 simulations with refit = FALSE . See ?DHARMa::simulateResiduals for help. 
##  
## Scaled residual values: 0.564 0.62 0.228 0.512 0.6 0.58 0.968 0.008 0.056 0.504 0.004 0.776 0.564 0.604 0.516 0.564 0.628 0.468 0.052 0.58 ...
model1a |> testResiduals(plot=T)  

## $uniformity
## 
##  Asymptotic one-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test
## 
## data:  simulationOutput$scaledResiduals
## D = 0.28, p-value = 0.0007873
## alternative hypothesis: two-sided
## 
## 
## $dispersion
## 
##  DHARMa nonparametric dispersion test via sd of residuals fitted vs.
##  simulated
## 
## data:  simulationOutput
## dispersion = 1.0213, p-value = 0.832
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
## 
## 
## $outliers
## 
##  DHARMa outlier test based on exact binomial test with approximate
##  expectations
## 
## data:  simulationOutput
## outliers at both margin(s) = 1, observations = 50, p-value = 0.3297
## alternative hypothesis: true probability of success is not equal to 0.007968127
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.000506228 0.106469546
## sample estimates:
## frequency of outliers (expected: 0.00796812749003984 ) 
##                                                   0.02
performance::check_model(model1a, detrend=FALSE)
## `check_outliers()` does not yet support models of class `glmmTMB`.

Partial effect plots

model1a |> ggemmeans(~TEMPERATURE) |> 
  plot()

Model investigation

Summary

model1a |> summary()
##  Family: gaussian  ( identity )
## Formula:          HATCHING_TIME ~ TEMPERATURE + (1 | FEMALE)
## Data: egg_df
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##     100.7     110.3     -45.4      90.7        45 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance  Std.Dev. 
##  FEMALE   (Intercept) 4.058e-10 2.014e-05
##  Residual             3.593e-01 5.994e-01
## Number of obs: 50, groups:  FEMALE, 33
## 
## Dispersion estimate for gaussian family (sigma^2): 0.359 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                 Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)      10.9444     0.1413   77.46  < 2e-16 ***
## TEMPERATURE28.5  -1.4829     0.2182   -6.80 1.07e-11 ***
## TEMPERATURE30    -2.0497     0.1972  -10.40  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Anova

model1a |> Anova()

Confint

model1a |> confint()
##                                2.5 %    97.5 %      Estimate
## (Intercept)                10.667536 11.221353  1.094444e+01
## TEMPERATURE28.5            -1.910514 -1.055298 -1.482906e+00
## TEMPERATURE30              -2.436129 -1.663287 -2.049708e+00
## Std.Dev.(Intercept)|FEMALE  0.000000       Inf  2.014402e-05

r-squared

model1a |> r2_nakagawa()
## Warning: Can't compute random effect variances. Some variance components equal
##   zero. Your model may suffer from singularity (see `?lme4::isSingular`
##   and `?performance::check_singularity`).
##   Decrease the `tolerance` level to force the calculation of random effect
##   variances, or impose priors on your random effects parameters (using
##   packages like `brms` or `glmmTMB`).
## Random effect variances not available. Returned R2 does not account for random effects.
## # R2 for Mixed Models
## 
##   Conditional R2: NA
##      Marginal R2: 0.698

Post-hoc analysis

model1a |> emmeans(~TEMPERATURE, type ="response")
##  TEMPERATURE emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  27           10.94 0.141 Inf     10.67     11.22
##  28.5          9.46 0.166 Inf      9.14      9.79
##  30            8.89 0.138 Inf      8.63      9.16
## 
## Confidence level used: 0.95
model1a |> emmeans(~TEMPERATURE, type ="response") |> pairs() |> summary()

Summary figure

egg_df <- egg_df |> drop_na(HATCHING_TIME)

time.obs <- egg_df |> 
  mutate(Pred =predict(model1a, re.form=NULL, type ='response'), 
         Resid =residuals(model1a, type ='response'), 
         Fit =Pred+Resid) 

time.obs.summarize <-  time.obs |> 
  group_by(FEMALE, TEMPERATURE) |> 
  summarise(mean.time =mean(Fit, na.rm=TRUE), 
            mean.mass.female =mean(MASS_FEMALE, na.rm = TRUE), 
            min.time =min(Fit, na.rm=TRUE), 
            max.time =max(Fit, na.rm=TRUE),
            sd.time =sd(Fit, na.rm =TRUE), 
            n.time = n()) |> 
  mutate(se.time = sd.time / sqrt(n.time), 
         lower.ci.time =mean.time - qt(1-(0.05/2), n.time -1) * se.time, 
         upper.ci.time =mean.time + qt(1-(0.05/2), n.time -1) * se.time)|> 
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has regrouped the output.
## ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by FEMALE and TEMPERATURE.
## ℹ Output is grouped by FEMALE.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(FEMALE, TEMPERATURE))` for per-operation grouping
##   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.
time.obs.summarize2 <- time.obs.summarize |> 
  group_by(TEMPERATURE) |> 
  mutate(temp_mean =mean(mean.time), 
         temp_sd =sd(mean.time)) |> 
  distinct(temp_mean, .keep_all = TRUE) 

time.plot2 <- ggplot(time.obs.summarize, aes(x=TEMPERATURE, y=mean.time, color=TEMPERATURE)) + 
  geom_pointrange(data=time.obs.summarize2, 
                  aes(x=TEMPERATURE, 
                      y=temp_mean, 
                      ymin=temp_mean - temp_sd, 
                      ymax=temp_mean + temp_sd), 
                  size = 1) + 
  geom_jitter(width=0.05, 
              alpha=0.3) +  
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#69d7d8","#ff9c56", "#903146")) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values =c("#69d7d8","#ff9c56", "#903146")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(7,13), breaks=seq(7, 13, 1))+
  theme_classic() + 
  ylab("") + 
  theme(legend.position = c(0.8,0.9), 
                                legend.box.background = element_rect(color = "black", size=2)); time.plot2
